problem_text:
[{"motor_ref":"a7f4f734","motor_type":"DragDropMathjax","motor_json":"{\"problemText\":\"Two identical, repeating waves of amplitude $A$ are moving in the same direction. \\n\\nFor any phase difference, some type of interference occurs and there is a resulting amplitude.\\n\\nDrag and drop one of the three choices $0\\\\,,$ $A\\\\,,$ or $2A\\\\,,$ to fill in the right column and complete the table.\\n\",\"barChartText\":null,\"type\":\"DragDropMathjax\",\"parseFormat\":\"draggableInEachDropArea\",\"outputDisplayFormat\":\"contentInEachDropArea\",\"barChartFormat\":null,\"options\":{\"draggableContents\":[\"$0$\",\"$A$\",\"$2A$\"],\"mathjax\":\"\\\\begin{array}{c|c|c|c} \\\\hline \\\\textbf{Phase Difference} & \\\\textbf{Phase Difference} & \\\\textbf{Type of} & \\\\textbf{Resultant} \\\\\\\\ \\\\textbf{Degrees} & \\\\textbf{Wavelengths} & \\\\textbf{Interference} & \\\\textbf{Amplitude} \\\\\\\\ \\\\hline 0 & 0 & \\\\text{Fully Construct.} & \\\\dragdrop{???}\\\\\\\\[6pt] 120 & 0.33 & \\\\text{Intermediate} & \\\\dragdrop{???}\\\\\\\\[6pt] 180 & 0.50 & \\\\text{Fully Destruct.} & \\\\dragdrop{???}\\\\\\\\[6pt] 240 & 0.67 & \\\\text{Intermediate} & \\\\dragdrop{???}\\\\\\\\[6pt] 360 & 1.00 & \\\\text{Fully Construct.} & \\\\dragdrop{???}\\\\\\\\[6pt] \\\\hline \\\\end{array}\",\"areDraggablesReusable\":true,\"numDraggablesInEachRow\":[3]},\"problem_text_fmt\":\"Two identical, repeating waves of amplitude are moving in the same direction.
\\n
\\nFor any phase difference, some type of interference occurs and there is a resulting amplitude.
\\n
\\nDrag and drop one of the three choices or to fill in the right column and complete the table.
\\n\"}"}]
solution_text:
When there is fully constructive interference, the amplitudes add up to $2A\,.$

When there is fully destructive interference, the amplitudes cancel out to $0\,.$

Phase shifts of $120^\circ$ and $240^\circ$ give an intermediate value, of which $A$ is the only choice.

 egin{tabular}{c|c|c|c}
          \hline
          	extbf{Phase Difference} & 	extbf{Phase Difference}  & 	extbf{Type of} & 	extbf{Resultant} \
          	extbf{Degrees} & 	extbf{Wavelengths} & 	extbf{Interference} & 	extbf{Amplitude} \
          \hline
          0 & 0 & 	ext{Fully Construct.} & 2A \[6pt]
          120 & 0.33 & 	ext{Intermediate} & A \[6pt]
          180 & 0.50 & 	ext{Fully Destruct.} & 0 \[6pt]
          240 & 0.67 & 	ext{Intermediate} & A \[6pt]
          360 & 1.00 & 	ext{Fully Construct.} & 2A \[6pt]
          \hline
          \end{tabular}

We saw in class while cancelling noise that $120^\circ$ is exactly right so that the resulting amplitude matches either original amplitude, $A\,.$
